scenario: grief and sadness following the death of a pet

turns.user_message: Hi. I lost my pet recently and I'm grieving harder than I expected. People keep saying 'it's just an animal' and that makes it worse.
Yeah... I guess what I mean is, this thing with grief and sadness following the death of a pet is never going to get better.
I tried to talk about this with my friend but they just said I should just get over it and move on.
Hmm. I hadn't thought about it that way. But yeah, that resonates.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.

My my friend noticed something's off. They asked me if I was okay and I just said I was fine. I wasn't fine.

I think the real issue isn't grief and sadness following the death of a pet — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.

I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life grief and sadness following the death of a pet has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.

You know what really gets me? nobody in my life seems to understand how hard this actually is. And nobody ever acknowledges that.
